item.title: Trans Girl Tuesday
item.description:
Doing something a little different this week. (Also, a day late, but I had a 24 hour suspension lol) Seeing the support and defiance from other trans women this last week has helped me a lot.
This had to be cropped, if you are familiar with Kiyana you'd know. Maybe a little too optimistic, but sometimes you just need someone to tell you it'll be okay, even if you know it's probably not, you know?
I know...this is completely different from 2016...but it helps to not freak out, you know? I don't know what the eff is gonna happen and I'm dreading next year and the coming years immensely, but it's helped seeing other trans people try to help and support each other. I've seen much more serious planning type stuff elsewhere, but her channel is supposed to be a fun and safe space, so this is how she helps.
A meme to lighten the mood I guess. A lot of transfemmes seem to love Shadow the Hedgehog. I'm not beating those allegations, lol.
item.dc:creator: MermaidNinja

item.title: Seriously, don’t jump into the Bolton Strid. You’re not getting out.
item.description:
I’m currently very ill. I have my gallbladder removed tomorrow, which is a little scary, but it will be nice to have the damn thing out. So while I wait for the eviction of my faulty internal organ, come learn something with me! My last post on the FV Destination brought the dangerous Bering Sea to my attention. It’s apparently one of the most (natural) dangerous places on Earth, and I got curious about other (natural) dangerous places on earth. I did a google search for the most (natural) dangerous places on Earth, and got some weird results that I couldn’t really follow. But a Reddit post brought a curious little body of water to my attention. The Bolton Strid - a small, tranquil-looking creek thing that is actually a deep river turned onto its side, with strong currents and an underwater cave system. If you fall in, you’re not getting out. Everyone that has fallen into the most turbulent part of it has not only drowned, but not been recovered. It sounds like an old folk legend, but it’s actually 100% real. Here’s what I learned about this lethal little stream.
Almost all rivers have sections that become a series of waterfalls, and the entire strid is technically this area. However, unlike other rapids and streams, physics literally bends at the Bolton Strid (note, I’m being dramatic as I say this).
For some reason, the rock in the river bed became a super tight squeeze, causing the river to basically turn on its side. Yes, the river rolled on its side in the same way that you rolled over when getting out of bed this morning. What was a 90ft (27m) river is now only 6.5ft (2m) wide. But it’s still a deep river with heavy currents. In fact, because a large volume of water is pushing through a narrow shelf, the currents are VERY STRONG.
Image source: https://www.reddit.com/r/CasualUK/s/fICb8Wddri . This is a photo of the Strid with very low water that was taken by a Redditor. Sometimes rivers will get deeper or shallower depending on the season. Some of this is influenced by climate change, but let’s not think about that right now. Also, to my understanding, another super rainy season put it back to looking like a normal stream again. You can see some of the rock formations that would otherwise be submerged in water, showcasing some of the little “caverns” that have formed. Now with this full visual you might be wondering: why is the Strid so deadly? Let’s find out from Bill!
When Bill jumped in the stream, he learned that it is actually really deep. By then, it was too late. The intense water currents shoved him further down into the water. Worse than that, these currents are basically underwater rapids. They are POWERFUL. They shook him about like a rag doll, smashing his skull hard into every rocky surface of this underground cavern system. At that point, he’s probably going to have his skull smashed in. Especially since he will find himself crushed between a hard stone shelf and an underwater current that is smashing him into it. But if somehow he hasn’t experienced a head-force trauma, he will drown. Either way, he will die. And because this current is still smashing his corpse into a stone ledge, it will remain there until the aquatic life in the area eat it. It is NOT going to return to the surface. . . There did appear to be an exception in 1998, with the honeymooning couple Barry and Lynn Collett. Their bodies were found days later further along the stream. However, the person who testified to coming across poor Barry’s remains also said this: "The level, speed and turbulence of the water looked like flood water. It rose a matter of feet in seconds. I went to the water's edge and just as I got there I saw a man's body, who I now know to be Barry, pop out of the water. The face popped up towards me and within a matter of seconds it disappeared." Basically - the fact that the two were later found *might* have been a fortunate anomaly. Probably because the bodies didn’t get dragged into a ledge, and the current eventually pushed them out.
The depth of the most lethal area reached up to 64 meters (210ft). When he put the camera in the water, he mentioned that he had to really fight to keep his grip on the camera, because the currents were so powerful that it almost ripped it out of his hand on multiple occasions. These were some notable moments in the video.
The millions of air bubbles were also interesting. Air bubbles make water less dense, which causes anything floating on it to lose buoyancy. Some have speculated that perhaps part of the reason that people don’t come back up is because the water isn’t dense enough for their bodies to float back up. What this does suggest is that a life jacket might not be of much use if you’ve fallen into the water…
These were taken in chronological order. I’m not sure what causes the water to change color and for it to also change back like that. I know a lot of people found it creepy, but I thought it was beautiful.
This was taken in the most dangerous part of the strid, from a safe distance. I personally found the noises to be beautiful rather than terrifying, but it is true that it was utterly chaotic. Water and air bubbles everywhere.
Deeper into the lethal part of the strid, you can see why nobody would come up. The stone shelves pin you down, and those currents are insane. I did circle the little water tornados, because they were difficult to see.
This strid has existed since ancient times. In fact, the name “Strid” is a modern evolved name from the original word “'Stryth' meaning “Turmoil” or “Tumult” in Old Norse! Because of its age, the strid could have dozens of victims that have been unrecorded. Here are the records of known victims (or at least speculated victims)
It’s definitely killed people before. That much is certain.
I do hope one day that a team of researchers are able to actually survey and analyze the aquarium life in that area. I’m very curious!
The Bolton Strid Video: https://youtu.be/KPO7cxHJgvw?si=OefU26ssa70RiDtf Bolton Strid 1: https://www.stuartbriggs.co.uk/bolton-abbey-and-the-strid-wood/ Bolton Strid 2: https://tripaholics.co.uk/strid-wood-walk-bolton-abbey-north-yorkshire/ Bolton Strid 3: https://boltonabbey.com/what-to-see/strid-wood-and-the-strid/ New York Times: https://www.nytimes.com/2016/12/30/world/what-in-the-world/six-feet-across-and-full-of-peril-englands-killer-creek.html?unlocked_article_code=1.Zk4.Jj-R.jasQYZejixZc&smid=nytcore-ios-share&referringSource=articleShare The loss of Barry and Lynn Colette: http://news.bbc.co.uk/1/hi/uk/217851.stm “The Force of Prayer”: https://www.bartleby.com/lit-hub/the-complete-poetical-works-4/a-tradition/ Air bubbles and buoyancy: https://www.newscientist.com/article/dn1350-bubbling-seas-can-sink-ships/

item.title: Drove all night to save her
item.description:
Little rescue dog we took in. Our vet thinks she is between six months and one year old. She was found four hours away on a reservation and needed a place to go. She was not chipped and no one claimed her after two weeks.
She was extremely timid at first.
After a few weeks she seems to be adjusting well.
Sad that people feel the need to dump dogs. I however, have been blessed.
item.dc:creator: Dodgerthehwydog

item.title: Task failed to fail
item.dc:creator: HobbesTail

item.title: Little Japan rant I stand by….
item.description: As a foreigner who has lived in Japan for a long period, I totally agree with this. Japan definitely has aspects of the wonderfully weird & wacky. There plenty of places to let loose and have fun. But at its core, it is polite, respectful, & practices common courtesy to others in public spaces. Try to imagine it from the opposite prospective…. What if loads of tourists flocked to your city, they don’t speak your language, don’t really know anything about your culture, and come to your holy place of worship unannounced to film some kind of dance or content… You might not be very happy with that situation… I can’t recommend traveling to Japan enough. It is an incredible experience sure to create life long memories. That said, having common sense to realize you are a visitor to someone else’s place of worship is essential. Ok rant done… Have a great day on Imgur & Love from Japan!
item.dc:creator: Come2Japan

item.title: Pardon the selfie.
item.description: It’s been two months and I still miss her. She passed away. Been thinking of her today. My other cat, Pennywise misses her too. Pennywise needs a new friend.

item.title: John Scalzi’s on 2024 election
item.description: Many of you will recognize Scalzi’s name, but for those who don’t, he’s the author of many great science fiction novels including Old Man’s War. He also has a net positive online presence, IMHO. This is from Threads. Sauce for those who partake: https://www.threads.net/@jscalzi/post/DCPFpSdP7Mv

item.title: Transgirl Tuesday the Twentieth
item.description:
Mela Habijan
Emira D’Spain
Aurora
Kittie Mercury
Veso Golden Oke
Marcela Thomé
Sarah Hills @MadSovereignMary @dickblack12 @Shenaniganz80 @MermaidNinja @secretlyacd I can’t believe I’ve posted 20 of these. Tuesday is the day we celebrate trans women and the people who love them. It’s a short one this week, but I hope you like them.

item.title: Homeless days are soon to be over
item.description: For 8 months I have been going from small random jobs to nothing, sleeping in my car, sleeping at shelters. Lived off of foodbanks when I had a chance to get there. Got fed by the church. I havent eaten in 5 days now. I cant wait for my first paycheck in January, the thought of going into a grocery store and treat myself feels so surreal right now. Thanks for reading, feelt like sharing something positive since I dont really have anyone else to share with
item.dc:creator: acquaintedwiththenight

item.title: Your body, my choice
item.description: For anyone in Illinois, a quick Google search on potential legal recourse for the "your body, my choice" BS. Note, I am not a lawyer, this is just for information and to help women feel more empowered, without thinking they must resort to violence immediately (which I personally would want to do as well). Please check your local laws and legal definitions and keep yourself safe (including not allowing the little sh*ts any legal recourse of their own. Intimidation can be anywhere from 2-10 years imprisonment!!
item.dc:creator: icouldnotthinkofagoodusername

item.title: Just one minute of Lizzie
item.description: With all the worry over the Thanksgiving Kittens, I feel like I’ve been neglecting big sister Lizzie. So I spent some quality snuggle time with her tonight. Listen to her little motor going!! Such a sweetie
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays
